scenario.summary: November close day 1 — 1,200 entries across 6 entities
scenario.situation: November month-end close, day 1. The Controller needs to process and reconcile ~1,200 journal entries across 6 entities before the close deadline (business day 4). Key items: intercompany eliminations between Shanghai and Houston ($2.4M component transfers), FX revaluation for EUR and JPY positions, Q4 bonus accrual ($890K), depreciation across 12 plants, and inventory valuation adjustments. Last month, the team found 3 misclassified entries on day 3 that required rework.
dataInputs: Sub-ledger data (AP, AR, inventory, fixed assets)
Prior month entries for pattern matching
agentSteps: Auto-generates recurring entries: depreciation for all 12 plants, standard accruals, prepaid amortization — 340 entries posted automatically with zero manual input

Processes intercompany: matches Shanghai to Houston component transfers against receipts, generates elimination entries, identifies a $48K timing difference (goods in transit) and creates the appropriate accrual
Runs FX revaluation: recalculates EUR and JPY positions at month-end rates, flags JE-9005 (JPY 12M FX revaluation) as unusual — 3x the historical average due to unhedged JPY receivable
Validates all entries against GL account rules, historical patterns, and materiality thresholds, catches a $12K entry posted to the wrong cost center (CC-310 instead of CC-300) and auto-corrects
Reconciles sub-ledgers to GL: AR sub-ledger matches to 1100 accounts, AP sub-ledger matches to 2010 accounts, identifies and resolves 2 minor timing differences
scenario.expectedOutput: 1,200 entries processed — 340 auto-posted, 820 validated and posted, 40 routed for human review with context. Intercompany eliminations complete. Sub-ledger reconciliation done. Close timeline: day 1 tasks complete by end of day (vs. typical day 2 completion).

stats.label: Enhancement in touchless continuous close by 2027
